original_abstract: Abstract. The formation of secondary organic aerosol (SOA) from both ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH)-initiated oxidation of α-pinene under conditions of high nitric oxide (NO) concentrations with varying relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity was investigated in the University of North Carolina dual outdoor smog chamber facility. SOA formation from ozonolysis of α-pinene was enhanced relative to that from OH-initiated oxidation in the presence of initially high-NO conditions. However, no effect of RH on SOA mass was evident. Ozone (O3)-initiated oxidation of α-pinene in the presence of ammonium sulfate (AS) seed coated with organic aerosol from OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene showed reduced nucleation compared to ozonolysis in the presence of pure AS seed aerosol. The chemical composition of α-pinene SOA was investigated by ultra-performance liquid chromatography/electrospray ionization high-resolution quadrupole time-of-flight mass spectrometry (UPLC/ESI-HR-Q-TOFMS), with a focus on the formation of carboxylic acids and high-molecular weight dimers. A total of eight carboxylic acids and four dimers were identified, constituting between 8 and 12% of the total α-pinene SOA mass. OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene in the presence of nitrogen oxides (NOx) resulted in the formation of highly oxidized carboxylic acids, such as 3-methyl-1,2,3-butanetricarboxylic acid (MBTCA) and diaterpenylic acid acetate (DTAA). The formation of dimers was observed only in SOA produced from the ozonolysis of α-pinene in the absence of NOx, with increased concentrations by a factor of two at higher RH (50–90%) relative to lower RH (30–50%). The increased formation of dimers correlates with an observed increase in new particle formation at higher RH due to nucleation. Increased aerosol acidity was found to have a negligible effect on the formation of the dimers. SOA mass yield did not influence the chemical composition of SOA formed from α-pinene ozonolysis with respect to carboxylic acids and dimers. The results support the formation of the high-molecular weight dimers through gas-phase reactions of the stabilized Criegee Intermediate (sCI) formed from the ozonolysis of α-pinene. The high molecular weight and polar nature of dimers formed in the gas phase may explain increased particle number concentration as a result of homogenous nucleation. Since three of these dimers (i.e. pinyl-diaterpenyl dimer (MW 358), pinyl-diaterebyl dimer (MW 344) and pinonyl-pinyl dimer (MW 368)) have been observed in both laboratory-generated and ambient fine organic aerosol samples, we conclude that the dimers observed in this study can be used as tracers for the O3-initiated oxidation of α-pinene, and are therefore indicative of enhanced anthropogenic activities, and that the high molecular weight and low volatility dimers result in homogenous nucleation under laboratory conditions, increasing the particle number concentration.
humanized_abstract: The effects of initial high-NO concentrations, varying relative humidity (RH) and aerosol acidity on secondary organic aerosol (SOA) formed from both ozonolysis and hydroxyl radical (OH)-initiated oxidation of α-pinene were investigated in the University of North Carolina dual outdoor smog chamber facility. SOA formation from ozonolysis of α-pinene under high-NO conditions was larger than that from OH-initiated oxidation. No significant effect of RH was observed. The O3-initiated oxidation of α-pinene in the presence of ammonium sulfate (AS) seed coated with SOA from OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene resulted in less new particle formation compared to ozonolysis of α-pinene in the presence of pure AS seed. The UPLC/ESI-HR-Q-TOFMS analysis showed that the chemical components of α-pinene SOA include carboxylic acids and high molecular weight dimers. Among them, 8 types of carboxylic acids and 4 types of dimers were detected, accounting for 8–12% of the total mass of α-pinene SOA. Furthermore, the high-NO condition enhanced the formation of highly oxidized carboxylic acids such as 3-methyl-1,2,3-butanetricarboxylic acid (MBTCA) and diaterpenylic acid acetate (DTAA in DTAA) during the OH-initiated oxidation of α-pinene. However, the dimers were only detected in α-pinene SOA formed via ozonolysis in the absence of NOx. The yields of dimers were increased by a factor of two at higher RH (50–90%) relative to those at lower RH (30–50%). Increased aerosol acidity had little effect on the formation of these dimers. In addition, the SOA mass yield had little effect on the formation of carboxylic acids and dimers in α-pinene SOA formed via ozonolysis. Thus, the dimers were considered to be formed via gas-phase reactions of the sCI formed from α-pinene ozonolysis. The high molecular weight and polar nature of dimers formed in the gas phase may result in their increased particle number concentration as a result of homogenous nucleation. Indeed, three types of dimers (i.e. pinyl-diaterpenyl dimer (MW 358), pinyl-diaterebyl dimer (MW 344) and pinonyl-pinyl dimer (MW 368)) have been observed in both laboratory-generated and ambient fine organic aerosol samples, thus they can be used as tracers for the O3-initiated oxidation of α-pinene, and therefore can be used to reflect the degree of enhancement of anthropogenic activity. The homogenous nucleation of the high molecular weight and low volatility dimers resulted in the increase of particle number concentration.
